title: Textbook
description: One catalogue entry. One JSON document per textbook lives in src/content/textbooks/<owner>__<repo>.json. Only id, title, description, authors and url are required; everything else is optional, and arbitrary future fields go under `meta` rather than at the top level.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema (editors may use it for inline validation).

id.description: The textbook's own GitHub repository in <owner>/<repo> form (e.g. example-press/ml-in-python). GitHub guarantees owner/repo is globally unique, so it is the stable id. The filename is derived from it as <owner>__<repo>.json; expand to a link with https://github.com/<owner>/<repo> when needed.

description.description: One-paragraph summary shown on the catalogue card.

url.description: Absolute URL of the externally hosted textbook site.

language.description: Language code, e.g. en, es, fr, de. Drives the Language facet.

software.description: Associated software, e.g. python, r, general. Drives the Software facet.

subject.description: Subject area, e.g. math, stats, cs. Drives the Subject facet.

meta.description: Open bag for arbitrary future fields (edition, license, prerequisites, ...). Ignored by the UI until something opts in to reading it.
